items.title: SEO impact after migrating an eCommerce store from Shopify to Magento
items.body:
My team will be migrating an eCommerce store from Shopify to Magento for a client. Over the past six months, the client has invested quite a bit in SEO improvements on the Shopify website. This effort included the creation of three SEO-optimized product landing pages and various technical SEO enhancements, which have successfully increased organic traffic, user engagement, and online sales.

As we proceed with the overall website redesign and migration, I'm concerned about the potential loss of SEO traction. Could anyone with experience in migrating from Shopify to Magento offer any advice to ensure that we maintain, or even enhance, our SEO performance post-migration? Specific insights on handling URL redirects, maintaining meta tags and descriptions, or any adjustments needed in Magento to align with Shopify's SEO setups would be helpful.

The new Magento site will use a customized version of the Porto template: https://www.portotheme.com/magento/porto_landing/

items.title: Having a problem to add wordpress in my client's cpanel account
items.body:
I need some help from you guys that have experience in using Cpanel...

So, I have a client that has the website there and I'll need to create 2 landing pages.

The dude who created the website there did it with code, and I don't know anything about it.

Fortunately, I saw that there's a possibility of installing WordPress in a subdomain and creating the landing pages there.

But, I saw that to do this we need the "WP Toolkit" or the "WordPress manager by softaculous".

The thing is that in this client's account, I don't see any of those options.

I searched on YouTube about this and I found out that we can install them by using the WHM (web hosting manager), I asked for access to it and my client told me that the dude who created this doesn't have access to that.

Looks like we have a Cpanel account without the WHM.

So, with all this, do you guys know how I could get one of the 2 options I told above to install the WordPress?

Thanks in advance!

I searched on youtube to see if I would get the answers, and I did, the thing is that another problem came.

items.title: Text files vs. text in database
items.body:
I'm designing a new site, deciding whether to keep the bulk of the content for each page in a text file, with just the file name in a database record for that page, or to keep the entire text in the database record as a string. The text is typically a few hundred to a few thousand words, with embedded markup to include photos or whatever, to be processed in PHP before being sent out as html.

I don't find enough enlightening discussion of this design choice online. What are the pros and cons of each way?

The advantage of text files, I imagine, is easy access to the file by ftp or other means, making fixing typos or editing the material easy w/o having to fuss with the database at all.

OTOH, keeping it directly in the db means I won't have to bother learning how to read text files in PHP 8P Seriously, what implications are there for maintaining the site, security, efficiency, and aspects I'm not thinking of?

items.title: Question regarding 301 redirects and rankings transfer time frame
items.body:
A few months ago i upgraded my free wordpress site to a paid plan.

The free site is redirecting to the new paid domain now. I'm still waiting on the rankings to be transferred to the new domain. My paid site is now in google search console. Someone suggested creating a new google account, adding my free site to gsc and saying my site has moved to the new domain to speed things up.

Does anyone know if: a> it will make a difference to how long it will take to transfer and b> why they would suggest making a new google account for that?

items.title: Can I include urls of other domains in sitemap.xml?
items.body: I own domain1.com and create a sitemap: http://domain1.com/sitemap.xml. Can http://domain1.com/sitemap.xml include a url: http://domain2.com/somepage.html? Note that domain2.com is not owned by me. After googlebot crawls http://domain1.com/sitemap.xml, will it crawl http://domain2.com/somepage.html later? If not, what is the best way to let google find http://domain2.com/somepage.html, except put it on some authority website?

items.title: merging domains into one
items.body:
I currently have a single website that serves the same content, but in different languages on different domains.

i.e. at the .de domain I have the german translation of the site and on the .fr domain I have the french translation.

In general this is fine, but it does cause some issues. For example I cannot be authenticated towards multiple languages (without having to add an over-complicated auth service like oidc).

So I have been thinking about moving everything to the .com domain, with redirects from the others to ex .com/de/ and .com/fr/. But what I am worried about is how this will affect SEO? Should I be worried about my ranking after doing this? even though I keep all the non .com domains and add a 301 Moved Permanently code to the new place.

also curious about if this could even help my SEO ranking? I would assume that it is clearer for ex googlebot to see that under the same domain there is the "same" content with different languages

<link rel="alternate" hreflang="de" href="https://example.com/de/foo" />
<link rel="alternate" hreflang="fr" href="https://example.com/fr/foo" />
<link rel="alternate" hreflang="x-default" href="https://example.com/" />

items.title: Why do external links on Google Search Console vary so much from month to month
items.body:
I keep an eye on external links to my website that google reports in its webmaster dashboard for my blog (technical tipps).

According to that dashboard, my website has a wildly varying number of external links. I normally publish articles on a weekly basis. Nothing that would attract a lot of spam.

Stats:

  • May 2347 external links
  • June 1789
  • July 1708
  • August 1185

How should I read this report? Or what should I make of this information? Why would the number of external links decrease continuously?

items.title: How to manage canonical URLs for multiple domains with identical content to avoid SEO penalties?
items.body:
I am managing a website that has several country-specific domains (e.g., .com, .be, .nl, .fr), but all of these domains load the exact same content. The primary version of the website is the .com domain, while the others are used to cater to different regions.

Currently, each domain has its own canonical URL pointing to itself, which has resulted in duplicate content issues and links being flagged as toxic by SEO tools. I want to avoid this problem while still keeping all the domains live without redirecting them to the .com site.

Temporary solution we're considering: We are thinking of setting all canonical URLs on each domain (e.g., .be, .nl, .fr) to point to the equivalent .com version of the page. For example, the canonical URL for www.site.be/nl/page would point to www.site.com/nl/page.

My questions:

  1. Should I point all the canonical URLs on each domain to the .com version, even though they are hosted on different domains?
  2. Will this prevent the .be, .nl, and .fr versions from being indexed by search engines?
  3. Is there a way to balance keeping multiple domains live while preventing SEO penalties for duplicate content?

Any advice on the best approach for this situation would be appreciated.

items.title: Creating an anime (videos) streaming site - copyright problems?
items.body:
My plan is to create a website that streams Dragon Ball, Dragon Ball Z and Dragon Ball Super episodes and offer a better service than competitors. I understand that hosting the videos itself is illegal, but when linked through other video sources that are out of my personal control, it is considered possible?

My question being, in general, will I get in personal trouble when I create such a website? I won't host or upload any videos myself. And will my website be viable from copyright infringements? I don't mind pushing the legal boundaries a bit, but I will not do it once I'm headed for a certain fine/personal problems due to copyright claims.

items.title: CSS direction property, actual page language and SEO
items.body:
I have a menu with a vertical scrollbar in my HTML pages.
This menu is on the left side of the HTML pages.
In order to have the scrollbar on the left side of the menu, I used a trick I found on the Internet: I added the CSS property direction: rtl;

The page language (culture) is either French or English. I am wondering if (Google) search engine bots can get confused because they have contradictory information concerning the culture of the website and if it can impact SEO negatively.
The direction property is obviously taken into account for rendering the page but it is also taken into account by (Google) search engine bots for referencing pages?

items.title: Duplicate content for desktop and mobile
items.body:
front end dev here.

Quite often the design team I work with produces desktop and mobile designs which are quite difficult to create responsively. While it can be done, I feel like I am using an excessive amount of my time on making this work.

I'm just wondering if modern search engines would score us down if I were to, for example have two footers with same content with a different html structure and only display one for mobile and one for desktop

items.title: How do I find out what is arbitrarily removing cookies on my website?
items.body: Having an issue where some of our cookies are being arbitrarily deleted in Facebooks In-App browser, and I cannot figure out what is removing them. One of the cookies is our session cookie, so not having that renders the site pretty much unusable in the app. Is there some way in Chrome's remote debugging tools I can put a listener on the cookie to figure out what is removing it so I can fix the problem? The website works as expected in every other web browser except the Facebook In-App browser and it's a very frustrating thing to troubleshoot and fix.

items.title: Need Help | Google deindexed my entire website after downtime – now none of my pages will reindex
items.body:
My website went down for a few days about a month ago, and Google deindexed all of my pages. The site has been fully restored, but I'm still unable to get even a single page reindexed despite submitting the sitemap and requesting indexing through Google Search Console. There are no robots.txt or noindex issues, and all pages return 200 OK. Has anyone faced this before or knows how to fix it?

The website is https://www.iposcanner.ai

items.title: Why Googlebot is crawling only one page?
items.body:
I am trying to understand why Google bot is crawling only one page only on my website.

At the Google console I registered a small XML sitemap with 9 URLs. Only one URL I marked as a high priority (0.8) and that changes on daily basis. This URL is /Home/Index

Based on this answer I removed all other sitemaps, and on Home/Index I placed several anchors to the pages that I wanted to be crawled too, and these anchors are changing each visit to the new anchors. And pages behind these anchors are having other several anchors, and so on.

What I am seeing, Google bot is crawling only /Home/Index multiple times per day and seems to be ignoring all pages behind the anchors. What might be the reason and how to change this?

I can also see that other bots are crawling those anchors.

items.title: Wordpress Domains & custom DNS : Can I use Wordpress Domains email forwarding with custom DNS nameservers?
items.body:
I'm using Wordpress Domains for a website domain and want to use Wordpress Domain's free email forwarding feature, but prefer to use a separate DNS management service (i.e. external name servers) for the domain.

When I try setting up email forwarding under Wordpress Domains, the MX record just shows up with Name as @ and Value hidden as "Mail handled by WordPress.com email forwarding. Learn more.".

Is it possible to use Wordpress Domains email forwarding with a custom nameservers? If so what's the MX Record Value to use?

items.title: Can I fix noindex errors in Google Search Console for webp images?
items.body:
Google Search Console is showing a noindex error for my images in the WEBP image format, and I don't know how to fix this problem.

  1. Does this type of noindex error cause any problems?

  2. How can I get this photo format to be indexed?

items.title: Is reaching out to YouTubers such a great idea?
items.body:
I had the thought over the weekend that messaging the creators of various YouTube videos, which were about ChartJS (another charting library), and asking if they would be interested in making a video about my web charting software, RGraph, would be a good idea.

Thus far, I've emailed about 10 creators but have had zero response. Absolute nada. I've still about 20 to go, but is this really such a good idea?

items.title: What are the advantages of an AI Gateway over calling LLM APIs directly?
items.body:
I'm working on an application that integrates with multiple LLM providers (such as OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini). Right now, the application calls each provider's API directly, and while this works, it's becoming harder to manage as we add more models and AI-powered features. Some of the challnges we're starting to face include: • Managing API keys and authentication securely • Rate limiting and usage control • Logging requests and responses for monitoring • Tracking token usage and costs across providers • Switching between models without changing application code • Applying consistent guardrails and access policies

I've noticed that some teams recommend placing an AI Gateway between applications and LLM providers instead of making direct API calls.

For organizations running AI applications at scale, what practical advantages does an AI Gateway provide over calling LLM APIs directly?

I'm particularly interested in real-world experience around scalability, security, observability, governance, and whether the added infrastructure is worth the operational overhead.

items.title: Why are Bing and SEMRushBot ignoring crawl-delay in my robots.txt file?
items.body:
We host a large number of sites that have a large number of catalog pages. We would like to slow down several bots because the traffic is quite excessive from those bots. Specifically, we get quite a bit of traffic from BingBot and SEMRushBot that we'd like to slow down. The information pages for both bots say that they obey the crawl-delay directive. However, despite changing the crawl delays for both, I am seeing no change in traffic even after several days. Is there something wrong with my file? (I put 60 into the SEMRushBot delay but I read that they only delay for a max of 10 seconds. I have seen no change at all after adding them to the robots.txt file).

User-agent: *
Disallow: /nobots/
Disallow: /products/features/

Sitemap:

items.title: Desperate issue with unresponsive registrar
items.body:
I do not even know who to contact for help with this issue. Please bear with me... I did contact ICANN and the FTC but no responses yet.

The registrar I have several business domains with, DNBUY.com, has been unresponsive for several months now. Crickets. I have used this registrar for many years and this is the first time they have been this unresponsive.

A couple weeks ago I received an automated message that my credit card was unable to be processed for a domain that was expiring. I was able to login but was unable to change my payment information, and only received a very cryptic error message on the page that was unrelated to what was being managed. This week, my normal login credentials are apparently no longer valid, and I cannot reset a password or in any way access my account. For several months I have left messages on their site but with no response whatsoever. There is no email, company address or any other means to contact them other than the online contact form.

I am more than a little concerned about being able to maintain my domains OR how I can get an accredited registrar to take over if DNBuy is defunct. I can't even tell IF they are defunct.

items.title: How to fix the "Video is not the main content of the page" error for text contents
items.body:
I have several pages with textual content (about 1500-200 words with photos) on my site, which rank high on Google in their keywords. We produced a video suitable for this content for each page that we want to place next to the text on these pages; so that the user can see both the text and the video.

Since about a month ago, when it seems that Google's algorithm has been updated, for all my videos, the error "Video is not the main content of the page" was displayed in the search console in the no video indexed mode.

Interestingly, pages with videos were also removed from the google text results! It means that we are not in the first 10 pages for the keywords that we ranked one or two on the first page!

In search console, the status of that page is indexed, but not the video. However, the page does not appear in text results or video results.

The page inspect status is like below:

There is no video schema on this page. I don't want this page to come as a video result. The same normal result is enough. In fact, the video is not the main content of the page, and that's why we don't have a video schema!

The videos were uploaded directly to our site. (self-hosted) and displayed with html5 player.

We only have the article schema for the pages. (As before, it was ranked. Now only a self-hosted video has been added to the page.)

Is there a way to solve this problem? Can I make Google understand that there is no need to index these pages as video result and that the same page as before in the results is enough for me?

Any idea or solution? Thanks :)

update 1:

In the search console inspect, All these pages are available to google and reported as indexed.

Update 2:

I removed videos from some of these pages and after one day (or several hours) the pages come back into the regular results. (in it's keywords)

items.title: Massive drop in indexed pages - everything getting stuck in 'Crawled - Currently Not Indexed' (no recent changes, protections removed)
items.body:
I'm struggling with a serious indexing issue that keeps repeating across several of my business websites that have over 10 million pages. (Big e-commerce projects that are custom-made).

Here’s what’s happening:

The site had ~2.5 million indexed pages (others are duplicates that have either redirects or selected as non-canonicals), but now it’s down to ~690k. (the drop is happening not in one day, but like losing ~300-400k indexed pages every 3-4 days). The problem is affecting all my projects, but not so dramatically.

Most of the dropped URLs are showing in Google Search Console as “Crawled – currently not indexed.”

We haven’t made any significant updates or content changes recently. (Because there is not match, we can update - it is an automotive parts site, their main information is part name, OEM/part number, and diagrams).

All anti-DDoS (Cloudflare) and security protections were disabled/removed, just to rule out crawling or blocking issues.

No “noindex” tags, no major canonical or robots.txt issues detected. (We are using a self-referencing strategy for product pages).

The problem keeps repeating periodically - sometimes one site gets hit, sometimes another, always the same pattern: pages move from indexed → crawled but not indexed.

Has anyone experienced a similar pattern or found what triggers this? Could this be related to site quality signals, server response timing, crawl budget, or something algorithmic on Google’s side?

It feels completely lost. This issue has a severe impact on the business I represent, significantly affecting overall traffic and profitability. Projects are old and have a pretty big crawl budget (over 1tb crawled every month if GSC info is correct).

Any advanced insights or ideas would be appreciated. Thanks in advance to anyone who takes the time to help me out!

I’ve already tried regenerating the sitemap multiple times and repeatedly requested Google to recrawl the site through Search Console. I also made sure that all pages have proper self-referencing canonicals, no broken URLs, no duplicates, and that the content is unique and as engaging as possible for an auto parts store - including part names, reference codes, and diagrams.

items.title: What is the best place to add the language folder in url from an SEO perspective?
items.body:
I have a website with multiple languages so we added the language folder after the root domain directly, and then each folder after that is localized to the same language. For example:

XYZ.com/en/blog/electric-cars/

XYZ.com/sv/blogg/elbilar/

Is that better or I should consider moving the language folder after the whole main folders?

Like:

XYZ.com/blog/electric-cars/en/

XYZ.com/blog/electric-cars/sv/

What do you think is better from an SEO perspective?

items.title: Is there a Javascript library containing a function to convert the string representation of a Bash associative array into a native Javascript object?
items.body:
I have a web application's settings stored in a Bash associative array at location /usr/lib/app/deployments/1/config.sh on its OPFS.

The file is very similar to this:

declare \
  -A \
  contract_address
contract_address=(
  ["1.0"]="0xabcdef"
  ["1.1"]="0xghilmn"
)

I would like to be able to read it into a Javascript object like this

{ "contract-address": {
    "1.0":
      "0xabcdef",
    "1.1":
      "0xghilmn" } }

so I was wondering if somebody had already written a Javascript function which does what I'm asking and I can use like this:


const
  _bash_js_compatibility_module =
    require(
      "bash-js-compatibility-module");
const
  _from_bash_ass_array =
    _bash_js_compatibility_module._from_bash_ass_array;
const
  _object =
    _from_bash_ass_array(
      _bash_ass_array_string);

More or less I'd say I'm asking for a Bash associative array to JSON converter.

I'd like the function and the library to be licensed under AGPL3 so I can use them in the aforementioned web application. I think such a requirement probably makes impossible to publish the function's code on SE without infringing the license terms, so correct answers should just link the code.

The question has been moved to Software Recommendations SE as requested by an anonymous moderator.

items.title: Will my site be penalized in search engines for using data from a public API?
items.body:
I am developing a website that utilizes the CoinGecko API to display cryptocurrency coin prices. I would like to understand the potential pitfalls or common issues I should be aware of as I proceed.

The URLs on both pages are somewhat similar:

  1. URL on CoinGecko (site with API): /en/coins/bitcoin
  2. URL on my site: /coin/bitcoin

My concerns are:

  • Will I be penalized by Google Search for using similar URLs?
  • Is there a risk of penalization for using CoinGecko's data on my website?

items.title: Can Google Ad Manager be set up to not have any 3rd party ads
items.body:
I have a new client with an existing website. She has been using Google Ad Manager successfully for several years, except for one issue: she would like all third party ads removed. That is, she would like to direct sell ad space on the site to local companies only.

I have spend a couple of unsuccessful hours trying to find a way to turn off the ads from other agencies/3rd parties. My searches provide conflicting information.

  1. Search results on "What is Google Ad Manager" include the following: Manage Direct Sales: Negotiate and sell ad space directly to specific advertisers.

  2. Search results on "can 3rd party ads be removed from google ad manager" include the following: Protections (Blocking Controls): You can block specific 3rd-party advertisers, ad networks, destination URLs, and general or sensitive ad categories from serving on your inventory.

#1 seems to say that you could possible use GAM for just specific advertisers. #2 seems to say that you could block specific networks, but I have not been able to find the how part.

items.title: Why my website is not showing but my articles are showing in google
items.body:
I created a website around 2 months ago, and I have written around 90 articles till now. Most of them are getting indexed in google search. When I used to search the name of my website it used to appear in the search results below at the end of the page. But, from a week they don't appear in the search results and I have to click on search instead for options to see my website.

If anyone knows why this happens then let me know the solution.

I tried to fix it by adding the url in google search console and it shows that the url is live and is indexed. But, it is still not showing. Also I got a strange Page redirect issue and the page effected was https://www.mydomain_name.com/ , my actual domain is https://mydomain_name.com/

items.title: Setting a domain in Heroku and GoDaddy without www prefix
items.body:
I just purchased a new domain in GoDaddy lets call it example.com. I want to connect this domain to my Heroku app, so I went to the settings in Heroku and under Domains section I added the followings options for my domain:

  1. www.example.com
  2. example.com

Then, in GoDaddy, under DNS management I created two CNAME records

I used the DNS targets that I got from Heroku for each of the records. The wild card got the DNS target from example.com and the www got the DNS target from www.example.com.

Now, I wanted to test this setup so I tried to use GET request in Postman. I tried http://www.example.com and got 200 and the response I was expecting. However, when I tried http://example.com (without the www prefix) I didn't get any response but an error instead:

Error: write EPROTO 140275225778376:error:10000458:SSL routines:OPENSSL_internal:TLSV1_ALERT_UNRECOGNIZED_NAME:../../third_party/boringssl/src/ssl/tls_record.cc:594:SSL alert number 112

Is this setup that I mention is correct? If I want to support both of the domain with and without the www prefix what is the correct way to set this up?

items.title: How does proxy work in Virtualmin, and can I forward whatever.domain.example to an internal ip address with a different port?
items.body:
In Virtualmin, I've read that you can set up a proxy website under a subdomain.

All traffic on port 80 hits Apache where there are multiple virtual servers sharing the internal server IP.

I have a separate machine, a Raspberry Pi3 running a web service on port 8123.

If I create a subdomain.mydomain.example, can I get Apache to load the http://internalip:8123 site and send it to the requestor?

Is this what a reverse proxy is?

items.title: Charged for cancelled Google Workspace account, cannot access admin panel to make payment
items.body:
I have been receiving notices from Google about unpaid invoices for Google Workspace.

The email says to login to the Google Admin Console to make the payment. However, when I select my account on the login page, the login page reloads with no error message. This has left me with no method of repaying the owed amount.

For context, I started a free trial for Google Workspace from my personal Gmail account back in March. I used a temporary privacy.com card for the payment information so I would not be changed when the free trial ended.

The day I started the Workspace trial, I received a message indicating my payment information was declined and that all services would be suspended on April 3rd.

Unknown to me, despite the payment failure, the subscription continued until May 1st, when I received an email confirming my Workspace Business Plus plan had been cancelled.

I am aware this is an issue best handled by contacting Google's support team. However, any methods of contact I could find required signing into a Google Workspace account first, which I am unable to do.

Additional information:

  • The support article "Can't sign in to the Admin console" has a section about a potential error where accounts ending in @gmail cannot be used to sign in to the Google Admin console. However, my personal email was the only one that was ever associated with the account. This is confirmed by Google sending invoices with my personal email listed as the domain.
  • The previously mentioned article also mentions trying incognito mode or using a different browser if we have a correct username and password but cannot sign in. Neither worked.
  • Trying to access the Google Cloud Support Portal displays the message "Sign up if you don't have an account". The sign up link sends me back to the Google Workspace Signup page.
  • The "Contact Us" button in my workplace email requires me to sign in to the Google Admin console before I can use it.

How can I pay my outstanding Google Workspace fees if I am unable to access the Google admin console? Is there some way to contact human support without a functioning Workspace account?

items.title: How to implement permissions on Origin Private File System (OPFS)?
items.body: Apparently the Origin Private File System (OPFS) does not require web browsers to implement a file permissions API, so so I was wondering about possible ways to implement it at library level.

items.title: Blog posts are not being indexed in Google, while the main pages are indexed
items.body:
My website has been online for quite some time, and the main pages are already indexed by Google. However, none of my blog posts are being indexed.

Here is what I have checked so far:

The blog posts are included in my XML sitemap. I submitted the sitemap in Google Search Console. The pages are not blocked by robots.txt. There is no noindex meta tag on the blog posts. I have requested indexing multiple times through Google Search Console. The blog posts are accessible and return HTTP 200.

Despite all of this, Google still does not index the blog articles.

What are the most common technical reasons that could cause only blog posts to remain unindexed while the main pages are indexed?

What additional checks should I perform to identify the root cause?

items.title: How to combine Google Analytics and Google Ads tags into one script?
items.body:
I want to add Google Analytics and Google Ads tags to my website:

This is for Google Analytics:

<script async src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js?id=UA-xxxxxxxxx-x"></script>
<script type="text/javascript">
   window.dataLayer = window.dataLayer || [];
   function gtag() { dataLayer.push(arguments); }
   gtag('js', new Date());
   gtag('config', 'UA-xxxxxxxxx-x', { cookie_domain: 'my-domain.com', cookie_flags: 'SameSite=None; Secure' });
</script>

This is for Google Ads:

<script async src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js?id=AW-yyyyyyyyy"></script>
<script>
   window.dataLayer = window.dataLayer || [];
   function gtag() { dataLayer.push(arguments); }
   gtag('js', new Date());
   gtag('config', 'AW-yyyyyyyyy');
</script>

How should I combine them in one script to be added to my site:

Should I do this:

<script async src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js?id=UA-xxxxxxxxx-x"></script>
<script async src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js?id=AW-yyyyyyyyy"></script>
<script type="text/javascript">
   window.dataLayer = window.dataLayer || [];
   function gtag() { dataLayer.push(arguments); }
   gtag('js', new Date());
   gtag('config', 'UA-xxxxxxxxx-x', { cookie_domain: 'my-domain.com', cookie_flags: 'SameSite=None; Secure' });
   gtag('config', 'AW-yyyyyyyyy');
</script>

or should I do this:

<script async src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js?id=UA-xxxxxxxxx-x"></script>
<script type="text/javascript">
   window.dataLayer = window.dataLayer || [];
   function gtag() { dataLayer.push(arguments); }
   gtag('js', new Date());
   gtag('config', 'UA-xxxxxxxxx-x', { cookie_domain: 'my-domain.com', cookie_flags: 'SameSite=None; Secure' });
   gtag('config', 'AW-yyyyyyyyy');
</script>

Note: I have seen this question but it does not answer what I am after.

items.title: What about `llms.txt`?
items.body: We keep reading here and there that llms.txt is still not very useful, but it is a good starting point for better AI-bot search. Any more up-to-date information now that some big companies seem to have added llms.txts to their sites?

items.title: Is repeating the meta desc as a "page summary" a good idea?
items.body:
I've added a "page summary" to each page on my website. The summary for each page is the same as the meta desc - ie it's reused.

Is this such a good idea? Or will Google see it as duplicate and punish my website even more than it is currently?! (if that's possible...)

An example of this is:

https://www.rgraph.net/blog/2026/the-cdnjs-hosted-rgraph-libraries-are-now-available-and-up-to-date-again.html

Cheers.

items.title: Redirection of Spams hacked page 404 to 410 .htacess
items.body:
My website has been spammed by hackers for maybe 1 year. So now I have to update everything I think. And it seems that the page targetted by spammers is going to 404.

My question : I tested this command on htaccess to redirect 404 page to 410 but it's not working.

The spammed urls are like this

example.com/date-women..
example.com/need-women..
example.com/need-men-usa
example.com/old-women..

(Where example.com is my website.)

So I need to ask htacess to redirect 404 of these spams to 410

# If the request is not for a valid directory
RewriteCond %{REQUEST_FILENAME} !-d
# If the request is not for a valid file
RewriteCond %{REQUEST_FILENAME} !-f
# If the request is not for a valid link
RewriteCond %{REQUEST_FILENAME} !-l
# 410 gone for a long string of lowercase letters 
# and/or numbers followed by an optional long extension
# to handle spam URLs like /spstyaaliti4csf6ne.desiringly
RewriteRule ^/?[date](\.[women]?$ - [G,L]

it's not working?

I have about 500 pages to declare on Bing and Google Search Console. So it's better to erase them using 410 with htaccess.

Can you help ?

items.title: Google Ad Manager (not 360) no connector to Google Data Studio
items.body: Does Google Ad Manager (not 360) have a Google Data Studio connector? Their page here is subtly ambiguous https://support.google.com/admanager/answer/7558019?hl=en

items.title: Impact of geo-relevant backlink: Good or not really?
items.body:
Looking at building some backlinks and have a question regarding the geographic relevancy.

We have a website intended primarily for an Australian audience. When it comes to improving DA / DR, do we target Australian only backlinks? Or they can they from anywhere in the world?

The reason why I ask is because we spoke to two experts with varying opinions: one suggested it doesn't, a quality backlink from the US is a quality backlink for example. Whereas another suggested we should only target backlinks from Australian websites.

I understand from a direct referral / direct traffic perspective, we would want Australian backlinks, but from the view of improving search results, does locality or audience of the backlink matter?

items.title: How to recover Google Analytics Account From Trash?
items.body:
We accidentally moved one of our Google Analytics accounts to Trash, but now in Trash, we cannot see that account so that we can restore it.

Do you guys have an idea how to make it visible over there or have any contact email or phone number so that we can connect to the Google Analytics team?

items.title: Domain change, 301 Redirects, Site Map & Indexing
items.body: I changed my old domain name to a shorter domain name. I set up 301 redirects and uploaded the XML site map of the new website to Google Search Console. Google indexed the new domain, but I still see the old domain in the search as well. Should I delete the old website's site map from Google Search Console and keep only the new site map in there?

items.title: GA4 Unassigned Traffic
items.body:
I have a client using GA4 that is experiencing some anomalies in how GA4 is assigning Session default channel groups.

A large portion of their revenue (about 60%) is falling into the Unassigned channel group.

When applying the Session source / medium as a secondary dimension I also get (not set).

After doing some research this is the troubleshooting checklist I came up with as a starting point:

  • utm parameters
  • measurement protocol
  • verify GA4 set up
  • data streams
  • filters
  • referral exclusion list
  • GA4 config tag firing
  • audience triggers
  • Google ads related tagging issues
  • session_start event missing
  • Streaming events from other platforms to GA4
  • Cross-domain tracking
  • issues with analytics implementation

My question is two fold:

  1. has anyone else experienced something similar and what was the outcome?

  2. is there anything else anyone would recommend troubleshooting in addition to the above?

Any help would be greatly appreciated!

items.title: Key performance indicators to measure online brand image
items.body:
A company is going to launch an event and a series of online related content that will be published on it's website and promoted online and offline to it's customers and leads. The event will last for two weeks and related content will be promoted during that period a few days later. The main content types will be news stories, interviews, blog posts, videos, and photos.

The purpose of this event and content is to improve brand image among it's customers.

What are the best online Key Performance Indicators (KPI) that can be used to measure success of this initiative?

  • Results and conclusions must me presented a few days after the end of the event.
  • The measurement tools will be Google Analytics, Facebook Insights, and Topsy.
  • We don't want to create more than 5 or 6 KPI's, because a lot of KPI's produce results that are hard to communicate in a 10 minutes talk to people that are not web-savy .

Just to give you an example of what could be a possible KPI:

  • Increase in the number of visits, where the visitor spends 25% more time on the site than the average of the last three months. (Measured with Google Analytics)

It looks like there's a lot of room for opinion on this question, but what I need is not opinions, but links to web pages. I'm looking for specific information about documented best marketing practices used today.

items.title: Would linking back from an adult site to my main portfolio negatively affect rankings?
items.body:
I recently obtained a customer who runs an adult based nightclub, and built a website for them which has a very large amount of organic traffic from Google.

I would like to link from this website to my own to gain exposure, however would doing so negatively affect my search engine rankings?

The customer's website has adult based backlinks pointing to it from before I re-designed and developed it. Would rel="nofollow" be suitable protection, or is it best to avoid linking back all together?

This is the first time working with a customer in this industry, there is a lot to gain in organic exposure from having a link on their site, but I don't want to drop down my current Google rankings.

items.title: Automatic reauthenticate at oauth2 endpoint
items.body:
I am currently (ab)using an oauth2 endpoint of an API to enable users of the API to log in to my site (using response_type=code). I do not save refresh tokens or even access_token as that oauth2 endpoint doesn't support scopes and I want to minimize the dangers of stolen accounts.

The oauth2 endpoint does a successful redirect to my auth page (PHP) if the user already granted my client_id permissions and is already logged in via that service. This will happen without user interaction. My auth page will set some session variables and redirect to the appropriate page.

The session to my server could expire when the user just created a lot of data and want to save it (via AJAX). So, I want to automatically re-authenticate the user via the oauth2 provider (in case the session if it is still alive) without sending him on a series of redirects.

Thus the idea is:

  • If a once logged in user needs to be re-authenticated I create an image with an onload/onerror listener pointing to my auth endpoint.
  • My auth endpoint will then redirect to the oauth2 endpoint, which in turn redirects back to my auth site if the user already granted the permissions.
  • My auth endpoint updates the session and returns a single-pixel image if the authentication was successful. Otherwise not.
  • If the image.onload event fires on the client side and the image width * height == 1 the user is re-authenticated.
  • If the onerror event fires or width*height is!= 0 user interaction is required.

Are there any potential problems I may have missed and/or does this pattern exists?

As far as I see this way we would generate one more request to the oauth2 endpoint if the user session at the oauth2 provider is expired / the permission where revoked. On the other hand, if the user is still authenticated, we don't need any additional requests + the user doesn't need to load all the CSS+JS of the oauth2 endpoint.

items.title: Should I keep both old and new domains indexed during a 301 migration?
items.body:
I am migrating a gaming-related website from an old domain to a new domain.

Currently:

  • the old domain still has indexed pages in Google
  • the new domain has started getting indexed
  • 301 redirects are already configured

I am unsure whether I should:

  1. keep both domains accessible for a while
  2. fully redirect all pages immediately
  3. use canonical tags during the transition

I am also concerned about duplicate content and losing rankings during the migration.

What is considered the safest migration strategy for SEO in this situation?

items.title: Splitting an existing site into sub-domains and using a landing page with an HTTP 301 - is this just wrong?
items.body:
I currently manage a website for a large(ish) primary school. I'm in the process of rebuilding their website, and have had a thought today about how to potentially better organise the content.

We currently have a Joomla site running at myprimary.example, and most of the content and menu items are specifically related to the primary school. However, there are two menu items ("Nursery" and "After School Club") that I feel would do better with their own Joomla sites as they are technically separate organisations that are just closely related to the school (and also share the premises).

What I'm thinking of doing involves using 3 subdomains:

  • school.myprimary.example
  • nursery.myprimary.example
  • club.myprimary.example

I would then use myprimary.example as a landing page. In theory this page could display a menu for each of the subdomains, but ideally I'd like it to just redirect to school.myprimary.example. This would allow people to carry on using the domain in the same way as they have been doing in the past, and I would expect that the majority of visitors will be aiming for the actual school information. Each subdomain would contain links to the other two sites in a kind of "Visit our Other Sites" kind of a fashion, and would be visibly similar to each other whilst retaining elemenets of individuality (colour schemes, etc).

Another benefit of doing this is that if school is closed due to snow, I can switch to serving a completely static HTML page from myprimary.example explaining the situation, alleviating some of the strain caused by thousands of visits within a very short timeframe on days when the weather is questionable.

A separate point to note is that we currently use a CNAME record to handle the www subdomain. Whilst I don't see this causing a problem with the landing page, we may have to train people to not try and use www.nursery.myprimary.example.

Is this approach something that makes sense, both in terms of SEO and usability? Or should I just ditch this idea now before I get stuck into it?

items.title: How do I install a wildcard SSL certificate on WHM using Let's Encrypt plugin and Cloudflare DNS?
items.body:
I am using cPanel 92 on CentOS 7 and my domain.com's DNS zone is running on Cloudflare (free Cloudflare tier) and I'm having trouble getting my subdomains (of a subdomain) running over HTTPS. Security isn't important as no sensitive information is involved, but user trust is paramount so I can't have security warnings.

I have:

  • Configured a wildcard subdomain (of a subdomain) on a user on cPanel (*.tech.domain.com)
  • Installed the Let's Encrypt AutoSSL provider on WHM
  • Created an entry in my domain.com's DNS zone on Cloudflare for '*.tech'
  • Experimented with enabling/disabling the proxy of the traffic in Cloudflare (often just leaving Cloudflare acting purely as a DNS zone)
  • Experimented with adding a page rule for *.tech.domain.com setting the SSL option to 'Flexible' as it is 'Full' by default for domain.com.

Unfortunately I haven't once managed to get any subdomain loading without a security warning. Often, the subdomain refuses to load completely due to 'ERR_SSL_VERSION_OR_CIPHER_MISMATCH'. Other times I've had it loading, but throwing a warning as it is running on a self-signed cert.

I've read that I may not be able to do it the above way because the active DNS zone for domain.com needs to be the one running on the server so that AutoSSL can add the appropriate verification records to the zone. I don't fully understand if this is the case because AutoSSL seems to work for other subdomains on the server that don't use the server's DNS zone.

I've tried the 'Generate a new SSL certificate and signing request' three times and I'm not sure if I'm now supposed to wait to hear back from Let's Encrypt? Apparently there is, or once was, an interface to review pending cert requests to the CA, but I don't see such an interface anywhere to check whether or not there is anything pending.

I don't care that much if the traffic is encrypted between Cloudflare and my server. I don't care that much if the certificate the browser sees is a cPanel one, a Let's Encrypt one or a Cloudflare one, I just want it to look secure for the end-user. I'm happy to manage this manually and upload a new cert (and repeat quarterly/annually) if needs be.

I'm a bit lost here as I'm not really familiar with how this all works, any advice/guidance would be appreciated.

items.title: How to create HTML meta titles and descriptions for multi-page lists or directories
items.body:
I've searched for hours and cannot find a specific answer to my question. If SEO page titles and meta descriptions are supposed to be unique for every page, but you have a list or directory with numbered pages, how do you handle the title and meta descriptions?

I've seen numerous sites, such as ROM sites and software sites, that have lists with numbered pages, and the title and meta description are identical for every page. Is there a trick or some type of code that allows those pages to have identical titles and meta descriptions?

The website has a section that lists all updates/versions and it spans several pages. It has numbered page links at the bottom (1 2 3...) that you can click on to go to a particular page within the list.

items.title: Does onclick animation affect SEO?
items.body:
We want to build the website with mind-blowing animations, but SEO is also important for us and I am wondering about the SEO effects in the case of CSS and JS animations.

Let's say we want to build the page with Gatsby.js or Next.js and add animations in Three.js. We know that the page needs to be loaded very fast, so we don't want to use animations on init. But let's say we want to have a switch button on the top of the website that will trigger the Three.js and CSS animations. Does it affect SEO?

Another example we will have a static website with no animations and a second website with the same content but with a lot of animations and no index tag (flexing purpose). So when the user will visit our website he will be able to see the link (no-follow) to the website on the subdomain with animations.

Any ideas on how to figure it out?

items.title: Should I optimize meta data for each URL in listings even the created by search filters?
items.body: On my website there are many products and each of them can be shown with many search filters. I made sure that they had canonical tags but should I change each page's title tag, meta description, H1, ...etc.?

items.title: Google Search Console indicated page was "Discovered - currenltly not indexed", then page disappeared entirely
items.body:
I had a number of pages that Google's Search Console had marked as "Discovered - currently not indexed" 2 weeks ago, and so I clicked "Validate Fix" in the hopes of having the pages properly indexed. Once Google told me the fix had been validated, the pages disappeared off that list, but also weren't indexed.

If I check them on the URL inspector, it now says "URL is unknown to Google". All the pages have links to them from the homepage (which is correctly indexed), and they're mentioned in the sitemap, which says it successfully discovered 21 pages. Despite that, it says "no referring sitemap detected".

I don't know if it's relevant, but the "See page indexing" button on the sitemap is greyed out. Additionally, these pages have been up for about 4 months now, having only had this activity now, 2 weeks ago.

items.title: Website effectively completely deindexed by Google despite reporting indexed pages and high average positions in GSC
items.body:
For the past 12 months, a website I manage has effectively disappeared from Google Search results. Our homepage previously ranked #1 for organisation name, city but now appears on page 4, with largely irrelevant pages shown instead.

The contradiction:

  • Google Search Console shows our homepage consistently ranking at position 1 for our brand name
  • Reality: Drastically reduced impression counts and actual visibility
  • No manual actions or warnings in GSC
  • Pages are being crawled regularly and show as indexed

Additional symptoms:

All website link changes for our multiple Google Business Profile locations are rejected immediately without explanation, suggesting some kind of algorithmic penalty or "shadow" spam classification.

Suspected cause - cross-domain content syndication:

We operate multiple websites within the same organizational network and frequently publish identical blog articles across sites. I've implemented canonical tags pointing to the primary source for each article, but suspect this may have triggered an algorithmic duplicate content penalty.

However: The affected site has many unique pages that should demonstrate its legitimate value to Google.

Current status:

  • All indexable content on the problematic site is now completely unique (no more syndicated content)
  • Issue persists despite these changes
  • Other sites in our network are unaffected

Additional context:

  • Non-profit organization
  • All domains use .berlin TLD
  • Multi-location business with separate Google Business Profiles

Has anyone experienced similar algorithmic suppression, particularly related to cross-domain content syndication? What recovery strategies have proven effective for domain-level algorithmic penalties without manual actions?

items.title: How Can I Improve the Crawlability of a Large E-commerce Website?
items.body: I have an e-commerce website with over 10,000 product pages. Google only crawls a small portion of my pages regularly, which affects indexing and ranking. What strategies can I use to ensure that all important pages are discovered and indexed efficiently? Should I focus on internal linking, sitemap optimization, or something else?

items.title: SEO implications of duplicate content inside <nav> element for UX use case
items.body:
Whilst it may appear this question has been asked before concerning mobile and desktop versions of navigation elements, it hasn't in this way.

It was established in the accepted answer to the below question that having duplicate content inside a <nav> with one visible on mobile and another on desktop (via breakpoints) was not likely to negatively impact SEO. SEO - Responsive Website and Duplicated Menus

On the assumption that the above is still the general consensus (please advise if not), then how about a slightly different setup.

  1. A hamburger menu that contains 5 major links and 5 secondary links.
  2. On mobile the hamburger menu is made visible via a toggle button.
  3. On desktop the five major links are also visible in a sticky nav bar. When the hamburger toggle button is pressed the sticky nav bar animated away and the same nav bar as on mobile with both sets of links is made visible.

See this image:

Stylistically this suits the user experience we want, however, it will duplicate the 5 main links inside the nav bar in code. The nav bar items and those in the hamburger menu will not be visible at the same time but the will use css transitions to fade the nav bar out. So technically there is a duplicate of major links here but it is not being used to cheat SEO. It is merely a design choice.

Is it too much of an SEO implication?

Code concept:

<nav>
  <div class="nav-bar>
    <a href="/something/1">Link 1</a>
    <a href="/something/2">Link 2</a>
    <a href="/something/3">Link 3</a>
    <a href="/something/4">Link 4</a>
    <a href="/something/5">Link 5</a>

    <button>Hamburger Toggle</button>

  </div>

  <div class="nav-full>
    <div class="nav-full-major">
      <a href="/something/1">Link 1</a>
      <a href="/something/2">Link 2</a>
      <a href="/something/3">Link 3</a>
      <a href="/something/4">Link 4</a>
      <a href="/something/5">Link 5</a>
    </div>
    <div class="nav-full-secondary">
      <a href="/something/6">Link 6</a>
      <a href="/something/7">Link 7</a>
      <a href="/something/8">Link 8</a>
      <a href="/something/9">Link 9</a>
      <a href="/something/10">Link 10</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</nav>

items.title: "Separate or combine issues in audit reports?
items.body:
I'm performing a technical site audit using Screaming Frog. I organize the issues into separate Excel files — for example, "H1 duplicates" in one file, and "H1 too long" in another.

The problem is that the same URLs can appear in multiple files. When a developer makes a fix (e.g., updates an H1 to resolve a duplicate), the data in the other file may become outdated and no longer reflect the current state of the page.

This can cause confusion. On the other hand, combining all H1-related issues into a single file could lead to overload — too much data, too many rows and comments.

From the perspective of a technical SEO -

Should I keep separating each H1 issue type into different files, or should I merge everything into one file despite the size?

items.title: WIX Pricing Plans - Taxes & Custom Plans
items.body:
I am newbie to WIX & have been working on WIX website that currently handles SaaS subscriptions (Silver, Gold, Custom) setup using Wix Pricing Plans and trying to understand the best way to handle taxes, custom client plans - recurring billing, customization etc.

We're trying to build a workflow where taxes are handled correctly, recurring billing is automated, custom plans can be sold privately, and customers have a consistent checkout experience.

Taxes: There are 3 types of tax setups in WIX Studio:

  1. Settings → Tax → Location-Based Tax

  2. Pricing Plans → Manage Tax

  3. Settings → Getting Paid → Taxes (I understand these are for invoices/quotes)

What is the difference between 1 & 2?

If using option 1 and configure GST/QST/HST by province, will Wix automatically determine the correct taxes based on the customer's billing address during checkout? If only single province taxes are configured, will customers from other provinces see no tax? How does this work?

If using option 2, this seems more aligned to show same tax rates regardless of customer location however it only allows single tax rate to be added to pricing plan. Is there any way we can rename it from 'GST/HST'?

Custom Plans Payment Workflow: There is a way in WIX where we can create a hidden plan but then we can't share it directly for payment to clients that falls under custom plans. What is the best practice in WIX to handle custom plans while also ensuring taxes are applied & invoices are sent after every successful payment (first payment or recurring auto-payment as per user's billing cycle) ?

I have explored some resources and have came across 'Recurring Invoices' concept in WIX. So every time a new client with portfolio outside standard plans on website request custom quote, business owners create recurring invoice & send them via email and rest it handled automatically via WIX henceforth? Or Is there any other way ?

Lastly, Is there any way to remove this yellow highlighted section from checkout summary page ?

Thank you in advance for help!

items.title: How does Google evaluate contextual links from Web 2.0 and community platforms?
items.body:
I manage a content-based website and focus on long-term, organic SEO growth. As part of my strategy, I sometimes publish helpful content on Web 2.0 platforms and participate in online communities where contextual links may naturally appear.

I understand that Google discourages manipulative link building, but I’ve seen mixed opinions about whether contextual links from Web 2.0 blogs or community platforms still provide SEO value when used in a non-spammy, user-focused way.

From Google’s perspective, how are such contextual links evaluated today? What factors determine whether these links pass any value or are simply ignored?

items.title: Base64 encoded data url in <meta name=thumbnail>
items.body:
Google says that you can specify a thumbnail image to be shown in search results by adding a <meta name="thumbnail"> tag to the head of the document. See https://support.google.com/programmable-search/answer/1626955?hl=en

However, it does not say what kind of urls are accepted in the content attribute. The example on their page is a http: url with no mention of alternatives.

Does anybody know if a base64 encoded data url is supported?

For example:

<meta name="thumbnail" content="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB2...">

items.title: Meaning of "Pop-up Ad and Prestitial Ad" requirement by Google
items.body:
There is an item listed under Mobile Ad Experiences by Google that seems to not fully align with the Better Ads Standards.

Pop-up Ad and Prestitial Ad

Pop-up ads appear on top of the main page after it has loaded, and block the viewing of content. Prestitial ads appear before the content of the page has loaded, can vary in size from full-screen to part of the screen, and prevent users from getting to the main content. Neither pop-ups nor prestitial ads are locked to be flush with any side of the screen.

This description is somewhat unclear, and I have two specific questions:

  1. Are there any types of prestitial ads permitted on a website's subpages, or are they entirely prohibited?
  2. What is the exact meaning of the statement: "Neither pop-ups nor prestitial ads are locked to be flush with any side of the screen"?

items.title: Updating a link yearly to the latest year page, and SEO
items.body:
I want to create a menu with a parent item called Yearly Recap and multiple child items for individual years. A new child item will be added every year.

The parent item, "Yearly Recap," will dynamically update its link at the beginning of each year to point to the previous year.

For example:

  • On January 1, 2026, "Yearly Recap" will link to page "2025",
  • On January 1, 2027, it will link to page "2026", and so on.

From an SEO perspective, is this a good practice? Are there any potential drawbacks or reasons I should avoid this approach?
